## Key Question

What operational lessons from the Austin Samsung and TSMC fab buildouts apply to new semiconductor fab commissioning?

## Core Findings

1. Austin Samsung fab Phase 2 commissioned 2024 with GAAFET 3nm production line; Phase 2 ramp took 14 months from groundbreaking to first wafer (Samsung public filing 2024)
2. TSMC Arizona second fab construction pulled forward to 2026 due to CHIPS Act incentives; first fab yield ramp slower than expected due to US workforce skills gap (TSMC investor day 2024)
3. Samsung Austin reported 12% higher commissioning costs vs. Korea reference fab due to supply chain friction and local materials sourcing constraints (industry analysis 2024)
4. Austin workforce staffing required significant upskilling investment; Samsung partnered with UT Austin for semiconductor technician pipeline (Samsung 2023 press release)

## Frameworks Reviewed

| Framework | Evidence base | Limitations |
|---|---|---|
| Samsung public filings 2024 | First-party | Limited operational detail; investor-facing |
| TSMC investor day 2024 | First-party | Strategic framing; yield data not disclosed |
| Semiconductor Industry Association workforce analysis 2023 | Industry body | US-specific; workforce gap data |

## Actionable Thresholds

| Metric | Threshold | Source | Signal |
|---|---|---|---|
| Commissioning timeline | 14-18 months | Austin Samsung | Below 14 months risks yield quality shortcuts |
| Cost premium (non-home-region) | 10-15% | Austin Samsung | Above 15% indicates supply chain intervention needed |
| GAAFET yield ramp | 12-18 months | Industry estimate | Leading-edge nodes take longer than mature nodes |
| Workforce ramp | 18-24 months | SIA 2023 | Skilled technician pipeline requires advance planning |

## Design Principles

- University partnerships: align fab commissioning timeline with technician pipeline from regional universities
- Local materials qualification: begin local materials supplier qualification 24 months before first wafer date
- Phased production ramp: 14-month commissioning before full production capacity; plan revenue model accordingly

## Key References

1. Samsung Semiconductor public filing, Phase 2 Austin commissioning update (2024)
2. TSMC Investor Day presentation (April 2024)
3. Semiconductor Industry Association, "Building the US Semiconductor Workforce" (2023)

## Programme Relevance

The austin-fab commissioning experience is the closest analogue for the brazilian-fab semiconductor startup: both are non-home-region greenfield fabs with significant supply chain and workforce challenges. The 12-18% cost premium and 14-month commissioning timeline are directly applicable planning benchmarks. The university partnership model (UT Austin / Samsung) is replicable in Brazil (USP, Unicamp have semiconductor programs). CHIPS Act incentive structures have a partial analogue in Brazilian PADIS/ROTA 2030 semiconductor incentive programs.
